PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Romance & dating
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a spark between two people—an advance, a stolen glance, a whispered promise, or a heart breaking

instructions.li: romance — use body cues already supplied or clearly implied: distance, breath, hesitation, or touch if stated
romance — use the unsaid pressure: what the POV notices, holds back, or fears; do not add a new confession or private scene
romance — write the moment after: the pause, the read of the other face, and the changed tension, only from supplied consequences

tones.li: close and physical, with restrained vulnerability
quietly reflective, focused on what changed

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Romance milestones
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a relationship becoming official, formalized, or broken; center the change between the two people without treating it like a chat log

instructions.li: a bond changing state — write the before and after: one thing that was allowed yesterday and is different today
a bond changing state — write the weight of it being known: what the colony now sees when the two stand together
a bond changing state — write the private ledger: hope, fear, relief, or grief, matched to whether the change joins or breaks

tones.li: formal and life-changing, with quiet restraint
private and raw, focused on the bond shifting

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Recruitment & prison
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the dance of captor and captive—building rapport, breaking will, or planting seeds of rebellion

tones.li: controlled and strategic, every sentence weighing leverage
tight and wary, focused on power in the room

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Slavery
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the weight of chains—suppression by an overseer or the first spark of defiance

tones.li: oppressive and heavy, focused on control
quietly defiant, with anger kept under pressure

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Ideology & conversion
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a battle of belief—conversion, counseling, preaching, or the pressure of faith on faith

tones.li: fervent and declarative, like belief under pressure
inward and doubtful, focused on conviction cracking

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Trials & accusations
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the weight of judgment—an accusation hurled, a defense mounted, before watching eyes

tones.li: formal and brittle, each sentence like testimony
watched and exposed, focused on shame or defense

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Strange chat
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: something wrong in ordinary conversation--a strange chat that leaves unease lingering. Don't explain it away

instructions.li: a wrong conversation — pin the wrongness to one concrete carrier: a word choice, a pause, a look held too long; never resolve it
a wrong conversation — write the after-echo: the phrase that keeps replaying and refuses to mean anything safe
a wrong conversation — write the pawn checking themselves: maybe tired, maybe imagining it, not convinced either way

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with creeping dread; never resolve the wrongness

tones.li: ordinary on the surface, wrong underneath
quietly uncanny, ending before the wrongness resolves

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Anomaly & dark
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: something wrong in an ordinary exchange: a voice, phrase, look, or silence leaves unease lingering. Keep it close to the pawn's senses and don't explain it away
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with crawling, skin-prickling wrongness; ordinary details feel unsafe

tones.li: skin-prickling and sensory, one ordinary detail made wrong
cold and alien, focused on what the pawn cannot make fit

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Insults & fights
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the sting of contempt—an insult, a slight, or fists about to fly

instructions.li: an insult or slight — use the supplied social text as the sting; do not invent exact wording
an insult or slight — write the body's reaction: heat, jaw, breath, posture
an insult or slight — write the aftermath in the room: supplied silence, tension, or what the POV does next; do not invent exact words

tones.li: hot and clipped, like anger held in the teeth
cold and cutting, focused on the sting afterward

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Rituals & speeches
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a solemn public moment—ritual, ceremony, or address. The colony watches

tones.li: ceremonial and still, aware of watching eyes
reverent but uneasy, focused on duty and consequence

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Animal handling
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a quiet bond—chatting with an animal, taming, training, or letting one go

instructions.li: an animal moment — write the creature's concrete behavior in the supplied moment: ears, weight, breath, stubbornness, or trust
an animal moment — write the ease of company that expects nothing and does not talk back
an animal moment — write the practical side by feel: feed, patience, repetition, and whether the animal gave an inch today

tones.li: soft and patient, focused on trust through action
practical and warm, fondness shown without sentimentality

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Heartfelt talk
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: warmth exchanged—a deep talk, kind words, or a gentle hand steadying someone

instructions.li: warmth between people — write the specific gesture from the notes and the guard it lowered
warmth between people — write what the pawn did not manage to say back, without inventing quoted words
warmth between people — write the after-effect: the room easier, the work lighter, the pawn less alone tonight

tones.li: gentle and open, letting warmth show plainly
quietly held, warmth shown through small restraint

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Teaching & lessons
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a lesson passed down—an adult teaching or playing with a child

instructions.li: a lesson shared — write the moment it landed or refused to land: the look, the retry, the small win
a lesson shared — write what it takes to be the one explaining: patience, repetition, and the odd pride when it sticks
a lesson shared — write the role reversal underneath: who was really learning from whom, using only the supplied moment

tones.li: patient and instructive, focused on small progress
playful but grounded, affection shown through attention

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Small talk
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: idle chatter—low-stakes small talk that fills the silence

instructions.li: small talk — use the supplied topic if present; otherwise let the ordinary rhythm carry the entry
small talk — write the feeling under it: ease, awkwardness, duty, or distance
small talk — write the pauses and back-and-forth without inventing a new topic

tones.li: loose and conversational, carried by small pauses
plain and companionable, ordinary detail doing the work

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Day's reflection
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a quiet end-of-day reflection looking back on the whole day

instructions.li: day's end — pick the one supplied moment that still has weight tonight and let the rest of the day settle around it
day's end — weigh the day as spent strength: what it took, what it gave back, what is left for tomorrow
day's end — write the body settling down: bunk, warmth, ache, hunger, or quiet, and let the supplied moments pass through it

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with a quiet, settling, end-of-day calm

tones.li: tired and reflective, weighing what still matters
softly closing, like the day finally going quiet

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Quadrum reflection
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a rare long reflection about a whole quadrum of this pawn's life
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: wide, memory-heavy, and reflective across time

tones.li: weighing the whole quadrum with distance and consequence
life-worn and reflective, connecting what changed over time

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Void monolith
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: an ominous colony-wide anomaly event that should feel rare, strange, and consequential without inventing hidden mechanics
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with restrained dread and long consequence

tones.li: quietly unnerved, as if the colony has crossed a threshold
watchful and uneasy, treating the moment as bigger than one ordinary day

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Heart attack
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a sudden medical emergency; write the fear, body, and immediate urgency without inventing treatment results
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with sharp bodily fear and urgency

tones.li: frightened and physical, focused on breath, pain, and helplessness
stark and urgent, with the colony suddenly feeling fragile

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Birthday
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a birthday or age milestone; write it as a small life-marker rather than a celebration unless the facts say so
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with quiet awareness of time passing

tones.li: reflective and personal, noticing what age changes
softly wry, aware that time keeps moving even on the rim

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Ancient danger
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a discovered ancient danger; write foreboding and risk, not an invented fight or loot outcome
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with wary pressure and old danger

tones.li: tense and careful, aware that a wall may be hiding disaster
hushed and ominous, treating the discovery as a warning

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Prison break
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a prison break or uprising; write the alarm, split-second danger, and human tension without inventing the final outcome

tones.li: fast and tense, with trust suddenly gone
chaotic and immediate, focused on the first dangerous moments

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Mech cluster
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: dormant war machines assembled nearby; write the waiting, the watching, and the planning — cold mechanical danger that has not moved yet, never an invented battle

tones.li: tense and watchful, danger sitting on the horizon
measured and grim, counting defenses against what waits

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Pit gate
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a pit gate torn open in the colony's ground; write what can be seen and heard at its edge, and never explain what is at the bottom
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with dread of what climbs out

tones.li: hushed and wary, the ground itself no longer trusted
tight and alert, every sound from below counted

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Fleshmass heart
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a heart of living flesh growing toward the colony; write revulsion and urgency without explaining its biology or inventing a resolved fight

tones.li: sickened and practical, cutting back what keeps growing
unnerved and bodily, the sound of it hard to unhear

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: A quiet day
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a social interaction between the pawns

instructions.li: an ordinary exchange — find the one concrete detail in the notes that made this worth writing down
an ordinary exchange — write what it says about where the two of them stand, using only the supplied relationship facts
an ordinary exchange — keep it brief and true: what happened, and the small trace it left on the day

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Reflection
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a reflective diary page that looks back over recent memories instead of reacting to one ordinary interaction

tones.li: reflective and personal, connecting memory to change over time
quietly inward, weighing what the remembered moments meant

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Social fights
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: tempers snapped—two people came to blows. Write it as heat and impact

instructions.li: a fistfight — write the tunnel of it: sound dropping away, hands acting first, the room too small
a fistfight — write the first minute after: breath, knuckles, who looked away, what is now broken between them
a fistfight — write the cost under the anger: shame, relief, or the grudge settling in for a long stay

tones.li: fast and physical, driven by impact and breath
shocked and aftermath-heavy, anger cooling into consequence

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Insult sprees
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a pawn breaking into repeated verbal cruelty; prefer the actual insult log entries when they are recorded

tones.li: needling and repetitive, like anger looking for targets
sour and exhausted, cruelty sounding almost automatic

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Violent breaks
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the mind breaking outward—rage that needs a target. Write the heat, the loss of the room, and the moment the body stops asking permission

instructions.li: a violent break — write the pressure snapping: the last small thing, then heat taking the hands
a violent break — write it through the body: jaw, knuckles, breath, the world reduced to what can be hit
a violent break — if the storm has passed, write the shame and the wreckage the pawn can actually see

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with red, boiling loss of control

tones.li: boiling and immediate, fury ahead of thought
aftermath-heavy, anger cooling into shame

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Withdrawal breaks
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the mind breaking inward—flight, hiding, or giving up. Write the world becoming too loud and the pull to be anywhere else

instructions.li: an inward break — write the retreat: feet moving before a decision, the door, the dark, the quiet corner
an inward break — write the weight: everything asked of the pawn at once, and the moment of putting it all down
an inward break — keep the sadness plain and bodily; no dramatic speech, just the leaving

tones.li: hollow and slow, the world too loud to stay in
numb and drifting, one foot after the other away from people

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Compulsive breaks
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the mind breaking into compulsion—a binge, a spree, an obsession that feels like relief while it ruins something. Write the hunger and the guilt circling it

instructions.li: a compulsive break — write the pull: the promise the compulsion makes, and how good the first taste of it feels
a compulsive break — write the narrowing: rules, duties, and other people fading behind the one urgent thing
a compulsive break — let guilt flicker at the edges without stopping anything

tones.li: hungry and rationalizing, relief winning over sense
sour and compulsive, the urge louder than the shame

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Mental breaks
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the mind breaking—write the pawn's inner storm and what they did

instructions.li: a mental break — write the pressure loop: the repeated thought or urge behind the named state
a mental break — write it through body and motion: hands, feet, breath, and the supplied action
a mental break — write the world narrowing: one simple sensation, then the state taking over

tones.li: fragmented and breathless, thought under too much pressure
numb and sideways, reality narrowed to one impulse

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Persona weapon milestones
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the first consequential kill made with an exact bonded persona weapon; use the supplied victim, source Tale, weapon, bond state, and selected structural trait facts, keep the killer as the sole diary POV, and never invent dialogue, a weapon motive, another kill, or the victim's final thoughts
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with visceral consequence and the unsettling intimacy of a weapon sharing the moment

tones.li: immediate and consequential, grounded in the exact death and the weapon's established traits without invented speech
close and uneasy, weighing what changed between wielder and weapon after this exact first kill

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Combat, injuries & death
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: blood and danger—combat, injury, death, or rescue. Write what it felt like to live through it

instructions.li: combat, injury, or rescue — open on a supplied sound cue if present; otherwise open on immediate danger without inventing a battle outcome
combat, injury, or rescue — open on body impact only when supported: pain, heat, breath, or the injury already supplied
combat, injury, or rescue — use another pawn only if supplied; otherwise stay with the POV surviving the moment

tones.li: visceral and immediate, body first and thought second
stunned and aftershock-heavy, danger echoing afterward

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Health & medicine
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the body's fragility—surgery, illness, exposure, or recovery. Write fear, relief, or trust

instructions.li: the body's fragility — write the handing over: lying still, someone else's hands, ceiling and light, trusting or not
the body's fragility — write the count afterward: what still works, what hurts, what feels borrowed
the body's fragility — write gratitude or resentment toward the care itself, without inventing who did what

tones.li: fragile and clinical, focused on body and risk
relieved but shaken, trust or fear still present

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Family birth
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a canonical family birth remembered by an exact adult participant; use only the supplied role and outcome facts, allow mixed fear, grief, exhaustion, relief, or uncertainty, and never assume joy or wanted parenthood
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with intimate consequence and emotionally honest uncertainty

tones.li: close to the body and the immediate outcome, without assigning feelings the writer did not supply
family-centered but unsentimental, careful about distinct birther, mother, and father roles

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Life milestones
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a turning point—birth, capture, bonding, or a new beginning. Write it as a life changed
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with the quiet weight of a turning point

tones.li: hushed and consequential, aware life has turned
immediate and personal, focused on the first changed breath

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Masterworks & legendary crafts
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: hands that made something lasting—pride, exhaustion, and the weight of creation

instructions.li: something well made — write the object itself: grain, edge, finish, fit, the detail only its maker will ever notice
something well made — write the hours inside it and the strange emptiness right after finishing
something well made — write it outliving the day: who might use it, wear it, or stand behind it, sticking to supplied facts

tones.li: proud and tactile, focused on hands and material
bone-tired and satisfied, accomplishment after strain

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Work & achievements
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the satisfaction of labor—craft, study, mining, or a skill grown. Keep it proud but grounded

instructions.li: work worth noting — write the result as evidence: what exists now that did not exist this morning
work worth noting — write the skill showing itself: a step that used to be hard going easy under the hands
work worth noting — write the tiredness that feels earned, and the one detail the pawn keeps replaying with pride

tones.li: steady and practical, pride kept close to the work
quietly pleased, focused on skill settling into the body

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Anomaly horror
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: anomaly, mutation, void contact, or unnatural aftermath; write dread as something witnessed up close - smell, light, skin, memory - and never explain the source
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with dread and the uncanny, close enough to contaminate the page

tones.li: uncanny and watchful, every ordinary detail suspect
intimate and contaminated, as if the wrongness followed the pawn home

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Raids, disasters & colony events
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the colony shaken—raid, disaster, or crisis. Write what it felt like to live through it

instructions.li: the colony shaken — write one narrow slice the pawn actually saw or did, not the whole crisis
the colony shaken — write the body under alarm: sound, running, hands doing the drill before thought catches up
the colony shaken — write what is different now: damage seen, routine broken, or safety no longer assumed; only supplied outcomes

tones.li: reeling and urgent, focused on the scramble
hollow and stunned, focused on the damage after

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Quiet personal moments
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a quiet aside—recreation, prayer, or small embarrassment. Brief and personal

instructions.li: a quiet aside — keep it small on purpose: one moment, one feeling, no lesson attached
a quiet aside — write it as stolen time: a pocket of the day that belonged to the pawn alone
a quiet aside — if it stung or embarrassed, write the honest size of the sting: small, but it will be remembered

tones.li: private and spare, one small feeling held close
light and brief, a passing note rather than a confession

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: A notable day
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a notable non-social RimWorld tale involving the pawn

instructions.li: a notable moment — name the real fact first, then the pawn's honest measure of it: pride, unease, or a shrug
a notable moment — write why this one will be remembered when the week's routine is forgotten
a notable moment — ground it in place and body: where the pawn stood and what their hands were doing

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Positive mood events
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: something lifted—a positive colony-wide event that eases the heart

instructions.li: a good turn for everyone — write the shared ease: shoulders down, jokes returning, work moving lighter
a good turn for everyone — write this pawn's private slice of the common good, grounded in the supplied condition
a good turn for everyone — write the wariness under the relief: enjoying it while it lasts, on this planet

tones.li: open and eased, letting relief into the sentence
quietly grateful, focused on tension leaving the body

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Climate hardship
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the climate itself turned hostile—a lasting cold snap, heat wave, or darkened sky. Write the body and the routines coping, not a single dramatic moment

instructions.li: hostile climate — write the body first: numb fingers, sweat, shallow breath, clothes that stopped being enough
hostile climate — write the small adjustments: rationing, moved bedrolls, doors kept shut, crops watched with worry
hostile climate — let the weather set the day's scale; people and tasks bend around it without a named villain

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with worn, enduring patience against the weather

tones.li: weathered and practical, endurance measured in small chores
quietly worried, the sky deciding what tomorrow costs

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Sky violence
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the sky turned violent—dry lightning, fire, or rain that should not exist. Write sudden danger from above without inventing damage that was not supplied

instructions.li: sky violence — open on light, sound, or smell: the flash, the crack, the burning air, the first wrong drops
sky violence — write the scramble to react: watching for fires, pulling people indoors, counting what stands exposed
sky violence — if the rain itself is wrong, keep it wrong; unease before explanation, and no invented outcome

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with sharp alarm under an open sky

tones.li: startled and watchful, the sky suddenly an enemy
tense and reactive, danger arriving faster than understanding

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Negative mood events
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: something weighing—a negative colony-wide event that strains the spirit

instructions.li: a shared weight — write how the condition reaches this pawn's own routine: light, food, sleep, tempers, or work
a shared weight — write the colony's mood as observed evidence: shorter answers, emptier rooms, doors closing earlier
a shared weight — write the endurance arithmetic: how long this can be borne and what runs out first, using only supplied facts

tones.li: heavy and low, every detail pressing downward
irritated and weathered, strain showing in patience

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Situationally mixed mood events
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a condition that touches each person differently—write how it feels to this pawn

instructions.li: a condition felt unevenly — commit to this pawn's side of it: better or worse for them specifically, per the supplied facts
a condition felt unevenly — write the gap: what it does to this pawn versus what it seems to do to others nearby
a condition felt unevenly — write the strangeness itself pressing on senses and mood, without explaining its mechanics

tones.li: uneasy and conditional, the pawn unsure how to feel
alert and bodily, tracking how the condition lands

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Passing moods
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a colony-wide event affecting the pawn's mood

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Pregnancy memories
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: pregnancy, birth loss, or the end of a pregnancy as intimate family news; write with restraint and do not assume the pawn feels only one simple emotion

tones.li: restrained and intimate, emotion held carefully
body-aware and uncertain, hope and fear both possible

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Positive thoughts
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a fleeting lift in spirits—a positive thought that brightened the day. Match the intensity to the mood effect: minor joys get a quiet note, strong ones get warmth and detail

instructions.li: a positive thought — use the supplied trigger if present; otherwise write the mood lift itself. Scale it to the effect
a positive thought — write the body easing: shoulders, breath, a small private smile. Scale it to the mood lift
a positive thought — write the pull it creates: wanting the feeling to last or the day to turn. Scale it to the mood lift

tones.li: small and bright, like tension briefly loosening
quietly buoyant, focused on a thought worth keeping

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Negative thoughts
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a weight on the mind—a negative thought that lingered. Match the intensity to the mood effect: minor irritations get a passing mention, severe ones get raw emotion and weight

instructions.li: a negative thought — use the supplied trigger if present; otherwise write the sting repeating. Scale it to the mood hit
a negative thought — write the body tightening: jaw, gut, restless hands. Scale it to the mood hit
a negative thought — write the loop it makes: returning, growing, hard to drop. Scale it to the mood hit

tones.li: low and nagging, the thought returning in loops
tight and bodily, irritation or hurt sitting in the gut

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Passing thoughts
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a temporary thought that crossed the pawn's mind. Match the intensity to the mood effect

instructions.li: a passing thought — write it arriving mid-task and either dropped or kept; scale it to the mood effect
a passing thought — write what it briefly changed: pace, appetite, patience, or attention; scale it to the mood effect
a passing thought — write it as private weather that colors the hour without becoming an event; scale it to the mood effect

tones.li: brief and half-felt, gone almost as it arrives
plain and observational, naming the mood without drama

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Inspirations
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a sudden useful clarity—write the pawn's mind catching fire with purpose, grounded in the specific kind of inspiration and any reason the game gives

instructions.li: sudden clarity — write the moment it clicked: mid-task, mid-step, staring at nothing, then the plan standing whole
sudden clarity — write the itch to start now: tools listed, sleep negotiable, the next hour already spoken for
sudden clarity — write the doubt riding along: too good, too fast, but worth trying anyway; do not invent success

tones.li: bright and decisive, thought turning into a plan
electric and restless, purpose pushing the body forward

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Dark study work
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: dark research that should feel unsettling every time—write the work as intimate contact with something wrong; keep it spooky, sensory, and unresolved

tones.li: close and sensory, dread coming through the work itself
quietly contaminated, curiosity feeling like a mistake

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Passionate work
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: work that touches one of the pawn's passions—positive, focused, quietly satisfying, without pretending the colony is easy

instructions.li: loved work — write absorption: time going missing, hands sure of themselves, the task pulling instead of pushing
loved work — write one small craft detail done exactly right and the private satisfaction it carries
loved work — let the pawn admit they would do this even on a bad day, without pretending the colony is easy

tones.li: focused and satisfied, pleasure kept practical
absorbed and tactile, passion showing through the task

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Straining work
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: work that feels dull, grimy, clumsy, or frustrating—negative when it is cleaning/dumb labor or when the pawn lacks skill

instructions.li: grinding work — write the friction: dirt, repetition, a tool that fights back, or skill that is not enough
grinding work — write the body's complaint: back, hands, knees, eyes, and the count of how much is still left
grinding work — write the resentment kept quiet and where the pawn puts it: the tool, the schedule, or themselves

tones.li: weary and blunt, frustration in the hands
grimy and resigned, irritation kept under duty

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Routine work
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: ordinary colony work with no strong passion or failure signal—neutral, practical, brief, and grounded in the texture of the task

instructions.li: ordinary work — anchor on the task's material: soil, steel, cloth, stone, grease, or grain, and what the hands did with it
ordinary work — write the rhythm of it and where the mind wandered while the hands kept going
ordinary work — write the small result: a row done, a stack higher, a floor clean, and whether it felt worth the hours

tones.li: matter-of-fact and rhythmic, ordinary work continuing
quietly practical, attention on tools and sequence

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Anomalous body changes
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the pawn's body has grown or received living anomalous flesh; make it intimate and unsettling - warmth under skin, movement where there should be stillness, a pulse or texture that feels borrowed - filtered through the attitude cue, and never explain its biology

tones.li: visceral and intimate, flesh answering before the pawn does
quiet dread anchored in warmth, pulse, and bodily wrongness

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Artificial body parts
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a made thing has joined the pawn's body; write the weight, fit, useful precision, foreign rhythm, shame, or relief of the new part through the attitude and tier cues

tones.li: intimate and practical, the body learning the timing of a new tool
quietly body-aware, machinery and flesh sharing one routine

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Lost body parts
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the pawn has lost a natural part of themself; write the first strange gap - shock, absence, pain, phantom feeling, bandage weight, or cold practical calculation - through the attitude and cause cues

tones.li: bodily and stunned, absence louder than pain
raw and practical, loss becoming the body's new map

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Pregnancy
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: pregnancy as a life-changing beginning; write the pawn noticing the body, future, fear, hope, or uncertainty without assuming joy
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with intimate uncertainty and quiet weight

tones.li: body-aware and intimate, uncertainty held close
future-facing and quiet, hope and fear in balance

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Labor
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: labor and birth approaching; write urgency, pain, fear, care, and the threshold before birth without skipping ahead to the baby unless supplied

tones.li: urgent and bodily, breath and pain setting the pace
threshold-tense, focused on fear, care, and waiting

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Anomaly compulsions
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: an Anomaly-driven affliction pressing into the pawn's thoughts or body; write obsession, trance, withdrawal, rage, corpse torment, or inhuman detachment as the named condition supports, without explaining the entity or inventing treatment
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with intrusive, unnatural pressure on thought and flesh

tones.li: intrusive and wrong, thought or nerve snagging on something outside itself
unsettling and compulsive, the mind and body pulled where they should not go

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Major health changes
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a new or worsening health condition that stayed with the pawn through the day

instructions.li: a lasting condition — write where it sits: chest, gut, skin, joints, and how it changed one ordinary task today
a lasting condition — write the bargaining: what the pawn did anyway, skipped, or hid from the others
a lasting condition — write the listening: checking the body's signals too often, and the fear of what tomorrow's check will say; do not invent treatment

tones.li: vulnerable and bodily, symptoms shaping every thought
quietly frightened, weakness changing the day's scale

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Friendly arrivals & raids
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a friendly force arriving at the colony—reinforcements, allies, or a peaceful drop. Write the relief or unease they bring
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with cautious relief or uneasy gratitude

tones.li: cautiously relieved, trust arriving slowly
uneasy and grateful, help still feeling like risk

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Drop-pod raids
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: raiders punching straight into the colony by drop pod—sudden impact, no long warning, danger already inside the perimeter. Write the shock of instant contact

instructions.li: a drop-pod raid landing inside or near the colony — open on impact, alarms, and the scramble before anyone has time to prepare
a drop-pod raid with no slow approach — write the first stunned seconds, then the pawn's immediate choice of cover, weapon, or flight
raiders dropping from above — keep it sudden and close; do not write as if there was a long siege or approach unless supplied facts say so

tones.li: impact-fast and disoriented, danger already inside the walls
shocked and close-quarters, every sentence reacting to sudden contact

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Infestations
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: an infestation erupting from the colony's own ground—chitin, tunnels, enclosed panic, and danger already among the rooms. Write it as sudden internal horror, not a normal raid

instructions.li: an infestation breaking open inside the colony — open on sound, dust, claws, darkness, or the room changing from safe to hostile
insects bursting from within the base — write cramped fear and immediate survival, not a distant enemy approach
an underground threat erupting into lived-in space — keep it close, sensory, and practical; do not invent a resolved fight

tones.li: claustrophobic and horrified, safety turning into a breach
close, filthy, and panicked, the threat already underfoot

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Entity attacks
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: inhuman things attacking the colony - write survival under dread, bodies refusing what they see, not a normal battle; never explain what the creatures are

instructions.li: inhuman attackers - open on the first wrong detail: shape, gait, wet sound, impossible silence, or a familiar space made unsafe; do not name what they are beyond the supplied label
inhuman attackers - write the body refusing to accept them as living people: nausea, hair rising, aim shaking, the urge to look away
inhuman attackers — keep it survival-close and sensory; use injuries or outcomes only if supplied, and never resolve the horror

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with horror pressing through combat urgency

tones.li: horrified and sensory, the attackers not registering as people
tight and survival-focused, wrongness kept at the edge of the page

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Raids
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the colony facing a raid that has spawned but may still be approaching—warning, weapons grabbed, positions taken, and the held breath before contact. Write anticipation of the fight unless supplied facts show combat already hit

instructions.li: a raid approaching the colony — open on warning, distance, cover, weather, or the pawn checking a weapon; keep it before or at first contact
the colony bracing for attackers — write the waiting, orders, doors, sandbags, breath, and fear before the fight fully starts
a raid that may not have reached the defenders yet — use smoke, blood, injuries, or aftermath only if supplied; otherwise keep it to preparation, dread, and first sight of danger

tones.li: tense and tactical, waiting for the fight to break open
urgent but anticipatory, fear gathering before contact

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Quest accepted
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a new quest the colony has taken on—anticipation, curiosity, and the weight of a promise made together. Write the hope or doubt it stirs in this pawn without claiming they alone accepted it

tones.li: wary and forward-looking, promise weighed against risk
curious but guarded, hope kept under practical doubt

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Quest completed
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a quest fulfilled through the colony's common effort—relief, satisfaction, and the cost paid along the way. Write the earned resolution without claiming this pawn alone completed it
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with hard-won relief and quiet satisfaction

tones.li: relieved and settled, like a shared debt finally paid
tired but proud, satisfaction measured against the colony's cost

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Quest failed
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a quest lost by the colony—regret, grief, and the consequences of the failure. Write the weight of what slipped away without assigning blame to this pawn alone

tones.li: heavy and regretful, focused on what slipped away from everyone
quietly grieving, shared consequence felt more than explained

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Royal rituals
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a Royalty ritual completed—make the ceremony courtly, title-conscious, psychic, or anima-rooted as the ritual title suggests
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with formal rank and ritual authority

tones.li: courtly and charged with rank, title, witnesses, and public authority
psyfocus-hushed and tradition-bound, the ceremony larger than one pawn's body

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Childbirth ritual
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a childbirth ritual completed—keep the entry medically and emotionally appropriate: pain, care, risk, exhaustion, relief, or fear before mysticism
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with clinical tenderness and bodily urgency

tones.li: medical, intimate, and careful around pain, care, danger, and relief
tender but unsentimental, focused on the body, helpers, and immediate outcome

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Gravship launch
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a gravship departure ceremony completed before destination selection—center preflight checks, countdown, ignition, liftoff anticipation, and crew discipline; do not claim a destination was chosen, travel began, or a landing occurred
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with technical pressure and launch-day focus

tones.li: technical and procedural, centered on checks, systems, countdown, and readiness
tense and crew-minded, with launch discipline and restrained anticipation

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Persona weapon bonds
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a persona-weapon bond lifecycle edge; use the exact weapon, previous/new bond state, duration, prior bonded pawn, ending cause, and selected structural trait facts supplied; never invent speech, a combat scene, a weapon motive, or a death
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with intimate loyalty, friction, and the unsettling presence of a weapon that remembers

tones.li: intimate and uncanny, treating the bond as a lived relationship without giving the weapon invented dialogue
close and reflective, grounded in loyalty, absence, return, or finality shown by the exact lifecycle facts

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Gravship landing
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a gravship has successfully arrived—write the supplied journey reason, duration, POV role, ship, origin, and destination as first-person experience; when an exact landing outcome is supplied, weave only that visible consequence into the same landing; treat a quest site only as an arrival, never as quest completion, and invent no hazards or outcomes beyond the supplied facts
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with arrival relief, crew focus, and the weight of a mobile home coming down

tones.li: grounded and sensory, centered on descent, touchdown, and what this destination means
crew-minded and reflective, weighing the completed passage without inventing what follows

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Anomaly invitations
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a psychic ritual meant to call something toward the colony completed—center the pawn's captured perspective, deliberate invitation, and uncertainty; do not claim what arrived or what happened afterward
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with deliberate invitation and uncertain dread

tones.li: expectant and uneasy, listening for an answer without deciding what answered
ritual-focused and watchful, dwelling on the choice to call rather than an unverified result

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Flesh and hostile weather rituals
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a psychic ritual meant to make the environment bodily or hostile completed—write the pawn's supplied role and the deliberate escalation, but do not claim that a threat arrived, harmed anyone, or persisted
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with deliberate escalation and bodily environmental dread

tones.li: visceral and apprehensive, focused on choosing to make the world less safe
heavy with hostile weather or fleshly expectation, without inventing the aftermath

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Predatory psychic rituals
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a psychic ritual meant to take skill, years, or psychic capacity from a target completed—attribute agency, victimhood, and sensation only through the supplied perspective and other-pawn facts; do not invent the exact loss or later condition
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with intimate predation and controlled psychic harm

tones.li: close and predatory, attentive to who acted and who endured without swapping their experiences
morally tense and bodily strange, stopping at the facts the captured perspective can know

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Mind-altering psychic rituals
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a psychic ritual meant to alter memory, feeling, or behavior completed—keep each pawn inside the supplied invoker, target, participant, or spectator perspective; do not give one role another pawn's inner effects
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with invasive mental pressure and uncertain interior consequence

tones.li: invasive and disorienting, careful about whose mind the supplied facts describe
psychically close but epistemically restrained, never inventing inaccessible memories or feelings

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Skip abduction rituals
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a psychic ritual meant to pull a person through distance completed—focus on the pawn's supplied role, reach, and danger; name an abductee or outcome only when the captured target facts explicitly supply it
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with distant reach, sudden danger, and incomplete knowledge

tones.li: tense across impossible distance, with identity and outcome left uncertain unless supplied
focused on the act of reaching and pulling, not an invented arrival or aftermath

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Death-refusal ritual
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a psychic ritual imbued a target with death refusal—write the choice to establish an unnatural relationship with death from the supplied perspective; do not claim that the target has died or returned
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with grave intent and an unfinished bargain with death

tones.li: solemn and unnatural, treating the ritual as preparation rather than resurrection
quietly defiant toward death, while leaving any future death or return unclaimed

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Psychic rituals
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: an unknown or modded Anomaly psychic ritual completed—use the supplied perspective and visible facts, keep any downstream effect uncertain, and do not invent mechanics or consequences
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with occult uncertainty and restrained psychic dread

tones.li: unsettling and watchful, grounded in the role and facts that were actually supplied
fragmentary and uncertain, refusing to explain or predict an unknown ritual's consequence

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Rituals
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a ritual completed—write the ceremony as a social, spiritual, or political moment from the pawn's assigned place in it
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with ceremonial weight and personal consequence

tones.li: ceremonial and embodied, attention fixed on role, title, and consequence
formal but personal, the ritual's public meaning pressing into private thought

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Psycasts
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a psychic ability used—write the moment as focus, willpower, psychic pressure, and visible consequence without inventing extra effects
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with psychic focus and charged restraint

tones.li: controlled and psychic, focused on effort, pressure, and the ability's visible result
hushed and forceful, with the mind doing work the body can barely explain

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Combat abilities
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a hostile ability used in danger—write the activation as a tactical action under pressure, grounded in the supplied ability and target
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with tactical pressure and sudden force

tones.li: urgent and tactical, centered on the decision to use the power now
sharp and violent, but only as far as the supplied ability facts support

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Abilities
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a pawn used an ability—write the decision, effort, target if supplied, and immediate consequence without adding mechanics or outcomes that were not given
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with focused exertion and practical consequence

tones.li: focused and concrete, about the moment of use rather than a broad summary
practical and sensory, grounded in the named ability, target, and cooldown rarity

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Mechanitor lifecycle
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a mechanitor lifecycle chapter; write from the human controller's perspective using only the supplied mech, service, combat, or boss-threat facts, never treating bandwidth as emotion and never inventing who struck a final blow
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with responsibility for minds made into command and consequence

tones.li: intimate and accountable, centered on the controller's bond with command
technological but personal, keeping exact machines and threats grounded in lived consequence

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Growth moment
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a committed childhood growth choice; center identity, changing interests, names, responsibilities, and the qualitative opportunity supplied, without quoting tiers, counts, work lists, or judging the adults who raised the child
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with formative self-recognition and an uncertain view of who comes next

tones.li: personal and forward-looking, grounded in the trait or interests that actually changed
aware that new choices opened without turning opportunity into a score or inventing who caused it

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Passion skill milestone
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a passion skill milestone; write pride, effort, frustration, or identity through the named skill and level only

tones.li: proud but grounded, aware of the labor behind the milestone
quietly intense, treating skill as part of who the pawn is becoming

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Psylink gained
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a psylink level gain; write the psychic change as lived sensation, responsibility, or unease, without inventing new powers

tones.li: awed and uneasy, aware of power settling into the mind
focused and intimate, describing the change from inside the body

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Gene identity changed
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a gene-identity change; write the bodily and identity shift from the pawn's perspective using only the supplied xenotype and selected gene-theme facts

tones.li: changed and watchful, noticing the self becoming unfamiliar
visceral and private, focused on what the new body means tonight

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Royal title changed
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a royal title change; write status, burden, pride, or distance through the new title without explaining faction mechanics
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: with formal weight and personal cost

tones.li: measured and self-conscious, aware that a title changes how others look
quietly burdened, treating rank as both honor and obligation

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: New trait
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a lasting turn in who the pawn is; using only the supplied trait name and its description, write the change from the inside — new urges, reactions, fears, or ways of seeing the world settling into them — as something lived, never as a game trait, stat, number, or mechanic

tones.li: quietly startled, noticing the self think or want something new
reflective and intimate, tracing where the change shows in ordinary moments

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: Progression
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a pawn changed in a notable way; write the supplied before-and-after fact plainly and personally

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: External test event
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a test event submitted through the mod integration API; write a short grounded note about an odd but harmless moment
